topic: Common Diseases in Rural India — ASHA Assessment Guidelines

diseases.name: Diarrhea / Acute Gastroenteritis
diseases.asha_action: Give ORS. Zinc tablets for children. Continue breastfeeding. Refer if danger signs present.

diseases.name: Tuberculosis (TB)
diseases.asha_action: Refer to DOTS center immediately. Collect sputum sample. Family screening required. NIKSHAY registration.

diseases.name: Malaria
diseases.asha_action: Do Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT). If positive — refer to PHC. Give ACT as per protocol. No chloroquine alone.

diseases.name: Acute Respiratory Infection (ARI) / Pneumonia
diseases.asha_action: Count breathing rate. If fast breathing — refer to PHC. IMNCI protocol for children. Give cotrimoxazole if prescribed.

diseases.name: Diabetes Mellitus
danger_signs: very high or very low blood sugar
diseases.asha_action: Screen with glucometer if available. Refer to PHC for diagnosis. Ensure medication adherence. Lifestyle counseling.

diseases.name: Hypertension
diseases.asha_action: Measure BP. Register in NPCDCS. Ensure medication adherence. Lifestyle modification. Monthly follow-up.
